DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-01-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME FOR AGED & DISABLED DBA JEWISH HOME & REHABILITATION CENTER's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Hebrew Home for Aged & Disabled's Demurrer to the First Amended Complaint is ordered OFF CALENDAR.

A party demurring to a pleading must meet and confer with the party who filed the pleading "[b]efore filing [the] demurrer." (CCP section 430.41 (a).) The meeting must be "in person, by telephone or by video conference." (Ibid.) The meeting must take place at least five days before the date the motion must be filed. (CCP section 430.41(a)(2.) The moving party must file a declaration with its moving papers demonstrating compliance with these requirements and/or that the opposing party failed to confer in good faith. (CCP section 430.41(3).) Defendant here emailed Plaintiff's counsel but failed to show he met-or made any effort to meet-in person, by telephone or by video conference. Due to Defendant's failure to satisfy the statutory requirements, the motion is ordered off calendar.

Defendant is granted 20 days leave to respond to the First Amended Complaint.

Plaintiff shall forthwith lodge a proposed order consistent with this ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-01-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME FOR AGED & DISABLED DBA JEWISH HOME & REHABILITATION CENTER's MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Hebrew Home for Aged & Disabled's Motion to Strike the First Amended Complaint is ordered OFF CALENDAR.

A party moving to strike a pleading or a part of a pleading must meet and confer with the party who filed the pleading "[b]efore filing [the] motion." (CCP section 435.5(a).) The meeting must be "in person, by telephone or by video conference." (Ibid.) The meeting must take place at least five days before the date the motion must be filed. (CCP section 435.5(a)(2.) The moving party must file a declaration with its moving papers demonstrating compliance with these requirements and/or that the opposing party failed to confer in good faith. (CCP section 435.5(3).) Defendant here emailed Plaintiff's counsel but failed to show he met-or made any effort to meet-in person, by telephone or by video conference. Due to Defendant's failure to satisfy the statutory requirements, the motion is ordered off calendar.

Defendant is granted 20 days leave to respond to the First Amended Complaint.

Plaintiff shall forthwith lodge a proposed order consistent with this ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/JMQ)
